reasoning: NVIDIA is the single most important company in AI infrastructure. The 28% gain and $78B Q1 guidance confirm the thesis is intact. The 21% pullback from ATH is a buying opportunity for long-term holders, not a reason to sell. At 2-3% of portfolio, appropriately sized. Jensen Huang's selling is programmatic (10b5-1) and small relative to his holdings. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Add on pullback to $140-150 (near 200-day MA). Trim 25% above $220. Exit if AI capex spending reverses.
exitConditions: Exit if AI capex spending reverses.
bullCase: NVIDIA is the undisputed AI infrastructure king. Q4 FY2026 blowout: $68.1B revenue (beat), $1.62 EPS (beat), Q1 FY2027 guidance $78B crushed expectations. Analyst consensus Strong Buy, avg target $256-268 (53-60% upside). AI capex spending still accelerating. Position up 28%.
bearCase: Stock down 21% from ATH. RSI oversold (28-39). MACD bearish. Jensen Huang sold ~$1B in 2025 (10b5-1). Tariff uncertainty, Iran conflict, Fed caution creating headwinds. Tech sector rotation out of mega-cap. Export restrictions to China.
